dod.preamble: Commit and PR the 0026-0028 setup. .lume/ state only, no engine/product code. Operator instruction: do NOT start any of 0026-0028 tonight.

items.text: No untracked review-queue setup remains under .lume/ (workstreams/gaps committed).
Workstreams 0026, 0027, 0028 have NO iterations (none started tonight, per operator instruction).
No product/engine code changed - the branch diff touches only .lume/.
Test suite still green (no code changed).
A PR is open against main describing the three queued workstreams.

check.cmd: gh pr view land-review-workstreams --json state -q .state | grep -q OPEN

handback: Committed the 0026-0028 setup on branch land-review-workstreams and opened PR #37. Scope: .lume/ only - objectives, plan items, 2 decisions, gap links (G11/G13/G15/G17/G19/G20). All 5 DoD checks pass, including the guard that 0026-0028 have zero iterations (operator: do not start any tonight). No code changed; pytest 427 green.
self_review: What holds: the PR is mechanically clean (diff .lume/-only, suite untouched), and the 'none started' instruction is encoded as a passing command-check, not just prose - so the operator's constraint is verifiable. Weak points: (1) approve/start silently no-op'd inside a combined bash block on the first attempt (the commit error aborted the line); caught by re-checking phase, no harm, but a reminder not to chain gate verbs with a commit in one shell line. (2) This is the second landing-vehicle workstream in one session (0025, now 0029) - the repo's commit gate means every batch of .lume/ state needs a working iteration to land, which is friction worth noting though not in scope to fix here. (3) Nothing here advances the actual review fixes; it only queues them.
